title: Refactor Finding
description: Structured schema for refactor findings produced by scan dimensions and consumed by owning agents. Defines the contract between the refactor observer and the agents responsible for remediation.

id.description: Unique finding identifier in format RF-NNN (e.g., RF-001)

dimension.description: Scan dimension that produced this finding. Determines which scanner identified the issue.

category.description: Classification of the finding type, used for agent routing and grouping

severity.description: Severity level of the finding. Determines priority and exit code behavior. critical/high → exit code 2, medium/low → exit code 1.

owning_agent.description: Primary agent responsible for addressing this finding

fallback_agent.description: Fallback agent if owning agent cannot or declines to address the finding

file_paths.description: List of affected file paths, optionally with line range (format: path:start-end)

description.description: Clear, actionable description of the issue. Should explain what the problem is and why it matters.
examples: Function exceeds 200 lines with 3 distinct responsibilities
Module imports from sibling modules creating circular dependency chain

suggested_fix.description: Concrete, actionable suggestion for remediation. Should be specific enough for an agent to act without clarification.
examples: Extract lines 45-80 into validate_input(), lines 81-120 into process_data()
Move shared types to a common module to break the circular dependency

acceptance_criteria.description: Verifiable conditions that must all be true for this finding to be considered resolved

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the finding

agent_response.description: Agent's response after processing this finding. Populated after the owning agent has acted.

reason.description: Explanation of the agent's decision, especially for partial/rejected/alternative outcomes

actual_changes.description: List of changes actually made by the agent

pr_number.description: Pull request number if changes were submitted via PR

resolved_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was resolved

metadata.description: Optional metadata for tooling and tracking

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was created

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was last updated

scanner_version.description: Version of the scanner/dimension that produced this finding

tags.description: Optional tags for grouping or filtering findings

related_findings.description: IDs of related findings that should be addressed together

epic_issue.description: GitHub issue number of the parent epic

effort_estimate.description: Estimated effort to resolve: xs=<1h, s=1-2h, m=2-4h, l=4-8h, xl=>8h

examples.description: Function exceeds 200 lines with 3 distinct responsibilities
examples.suggested_fix: Extract lines 45-80 into validate_input(), lines 81-120 into process_data()
